platform: XCP-ng 8.2.1, Xen HVM, Intel x86_64, 3-node cluster
domain_schedule: 3:1 (D0: 3 slots x 2ms = 6ms, D1: 1 slot x 2ms = 2ms, cycle 8ms)

failures.root_cause: E1000 RX interrupts do not fire on XCP-ng (irq=0 rx=0 on all nodes). The IRQ handler is never invoked by the CAmkES IRQ connector. HELLO exchange therefore never completes, and cross-kernel KRNLC traffic cannot flow. Local tests (1-11, 15a, 15b) pass on all 3 nodes because they do not require the network.
failures.hypothesis: Xen HVM E1000 emulation may require different IRQ setup sequence than upstream QEMU, or the CAmkES IRQ connector on XCP-ng does not receive the interrupt notifications. Needs investigation with a simpler polling-only test.

passing_tests_on_all_nodes: Test 15b: spanning revoke (no remote children since sessions failed)
note: On 2-node QEMU (docker), all 16 tests PASS (verified in commit 74a53a0). The XCP-ng result differs only in the E1000 IRQ issue — protocol correctness is confirmed by QEMU.
